subject: LU-1032 build: Add dkms support to debian client packages.

messages.message:
Patch Set 1:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/4405/

Patch Set 1: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/4405/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1: Fails

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.2/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/74526966-48e8-11e1-9c04-5254004bbbd3).  Ran 19 tests.  2 tests failed: conf-sanity, sanityn.

Patch Set 1: (2 inline comments)

I'm on the fence about this particular patch.  It seems to have gone through the motions of building the dkms package but in examining the resulting lustre-reviews build I don't see it anywhere.  It's probably a trivial issue and doing a build by hand might reveal where it went (if it was indeed built).

On the other hand, adding this patch doesn't seem to have caused any harm and looks like it's 98% of the way there so no harm in landing it as is.

Unfortunately, I don't have the time to do that build by hand and the debugging to figure out what's missing.  I highly suspect though that it could be that this patch is missing a hunk along the lines of (obviously, untested):

--- a/build/autoMakefile.am.toplevel
+++ b/build/autoMakefile.am.toplevel
@@ -186,4 +186,4 @@ debs:
 	popd && \
 	VER=$$(sed -ne '1s/^lustre (\(.*-[0-9][0-9]*\)).*$$/\1/p' debian/changelog); \
 	mkdir -p debs && \
-	mv ../liblustre_$${VER}_*.deb ../linux-patch-lustre_$${VER}_all.deb ../lustre-dev_$${VER}_*.deb ../lustre-source_$${VER}_all.deb ../lustre-tests_$${VER}_*.deb ../lustre-utils_$${VER}_*.deb ../lustre_$${VER}.dsc ../lustre_$${VER}_*.changes ../lustre_$${VER%-[0-9]*}.orig.tar.gz ../lustre_$${VER}.diff.gz ../lustre-client-modules-$${KVERS}_$${VER}_*.deb debs/
+	mv ../liblustre_$${VER}_*.deb ../linux-patch-lustre_$${VER}_all.deb ../lustre-dev_$${VER}_*.deb ../lustre-source_$${VER}_all.deb ../lustre-tests_$${VER}_*.deb ../lustre-utils_$${VER}_*.deb ../lustre_$${VER}.dsc ../lustre_$${VER}_*.changes ../lustre_$${VER%-[0-9]*}.orig.tar.gz ../lustre_$${VER}.diff.gz ../lustre-client-modules-{dkms,$${KVERS}}_$${VER}_*.deb debs/

Patch Set 1:

I also wonder if having DKMS support could be leveraged into getting rid of the whole modass build bits.  That is, don't even build a binary modules package and expect that everyone will install the dkms module and get their modules that way.

Or alternately if we'd still like to provide a binary modules package, build it with dkms instead of modass.

Patch Set 1:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.2/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/994b6140-48f8-11e1-9c04-5254004bbbd3).  Ran 23 tests.  2 tests failed: conf-sanity, sanityn.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(4 inline comments)

Guy,

This is really great work!  I am glad to see it finally getting done.  Thanks.

There's just a couple of things left and I think it could land.

Once this lands BTW, feel free to take a stab at replacing the modass build with it.  :-)

commit.subject: LU-1032 build: Add dkms support to debian client packages.
commit.message:
LU-1032 build: Add dkms support to debian client packages.

This change adds dkms support to the debian/ubuntu packaging
infrastructure.

dkms is a cross distro mechanism for the building and maintenance
of out-of-tree kernel modules. (http://linux.dell.com/dkms/)

The current debian/ubuntu kernel module package uses
module-assistant, which produces a deb which is tied to a specific
kernel version, requiring the user to manually rebuild packages
when the kernel is changed.

The dkms package will automatically build kernel modules for all
installed kernels. The package also contains triggers so that when
a new kernel is installed, lustre client modules are automatically
build for the new kernel. This reduces maintenance overhead on
client machines.

Signed-off-by: Guy Coates <gmpc@sanger.ac.uk>
Change-Id: I6b576881d2518fb2765b41b3a336e464f0101932
